at_a_glance: You have spent $5.92 across 10 sessions building Promptly from scratch to a fully functional macOS app. Session 2 was the heaviest at $1.30 — all of Phase 1 in a single burst covering 11 tasks including the phase gate review. Costs have been climbing slightly in Phase 2 as the app grew complex, but at $0.19 per task this session the efficiency is healthy and the finish line is close.

session_narratives.2026-04-18-001: Planning day — brainstorm, architect, and spec-review in one session. $0.61 for the full blueprint: BRIEF.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, SPEC.md, PLAN.md. The most expensive per-task session ($0.20/task) but sets up everything that follows.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-002: The big foundation push — all 9 Phase 1 tasks plus the phase gate review in a single session. $1.30 and the most expensive session overall, but 11 tasks at $0.12 each is respectable given how much groundwork was laid.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-003: F-STATE planning session — vibe-add-feature spec and spec-review for the state machine. No tasks committed, $0.47 spent on planning. A planning-heavy pattern that pushed cost up without committing code.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-004: F-STATE build — all 5 FST tasks shipped. $0.51 for the full state machine, DOM structure, CSS, and IPC wiring. $0.10/task average, efficient for the complexity.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-005: F-FIRST-RUN planning session — vibe-add-feature + spec-review. Again no committed tasks, $0.44 on planning. The pattern of heavy planning sessions before each feature is the main cost driver.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-006: Short F-FIRST-RUN completion session — FRN-004 and the vibe-cost report. $0.20, the cheapest session. Focused scope kept costs low.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-007: Design system overhaul + 6 bug fixes in one session. $0.52 to ship the dark glass aesthetic, BUG-002 visual fixes, and the mode dropdown native menu. High value for the spend.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-008: DECISION-004 (recording moved to main window) + BUG-004 vibrancy fixes. $0.42 to eliminate the pill ghost race condition permanently — a complex architectural change that paid for itself by removing the most brittle part of the codebase.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-009: The heaviest Phase 2 session — $0.68 covering vibrancy follow-up, BUG-007 (two sub-bugs), the FEATURE-001 splash screen, and opacity diagnostics. FEATURE-001 alone was $0.25. Cost spike was expected given the scope.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-010: Phase 2 close-out — BUG-008 system prompt overhaul, the full phase gate review ($0.31 of this session), and all 11 review findings resolved in one pass. $0.77 and every known bug + doc debt cleared going into Phase 3.

recommendations.title: Prompt cache not firing — every turn loads cold
recommendations.body: All 10 sessions show zero cache_read_tokens. CLAUDE.md is modified frequently (active feature section changes every task), making the whole file ineligible for caching. Splitting CLAUDE.md into a stable core and a dynamic active-feature append-file would let the 6KB stable section cache across turns. At this stage of the project the saving is small ($0.15/session), but it is worth noting for any future project.

forecast_narrative: Four tasks remain in Phase 3: error state audit, manual smoke test, build verification (npm run dist universal .dmg), and distribution prep. All are S–M sized with minimal file reads. Expect $0.30–0.45 to finish. Projected project total: $6.20–6.40.
